entry.title: 🚀 Launch Alert! Become a Data Architecture Powerhouse: Your Foundational Learning Journey
entry.content:
To equip data professionals with the skills to bridge technical expertise and strategic business impact, SAP Data Architecture experts and the SIX learning team are thrilled to announce the launch of the Data Architect Learning Journey (Foundational). This essential first stage of the Becoming a Data Architect curriculum is designed to empower aspiring architects, data professionals, and solution experts to master the core principles, modern paradigms, and critical frameworks that define successful enterprise data architecture.

 

🏗️ Structure: Building Expertise from the Ground Up

This learning journey is structured methodically, ensuring you build expertise layer by layer, from foundational theory to strategic impact.

The journey begins with Unit 1: Foundational Concepts, where you will anchor your understanding in the essential principles of modeling, metadata management, and architectural alignment. You will master basic terminologies and fundamentals, including Conceptual, Logical, and Physical data models, and understand how data architecture integrates with enterprise frameworks like Zachman and TOGAF.

Next, Unit 2: Modern Paradigms & Integration takes you into the contemporary world of data patterns. You'll explore and compare models like Data Mesh, Data Fabric, Data Lake, and Data Lakehouse, learning how to select the right approach for any scenario. This unit also delves into integration essentials, comparing and contrasting approaches such as ETL and ELT.

Unit 3 focuses on the critical need for Governance, Security & Strategic Impact. As data ecosystems expand, governance becomes the bedrock of trust. You will learn to establish comprehensive data governance frameworks (including stewardship and data lineage) and integrate crucial security and privacy controls to ensure compliance and secure modern use cases like AI/ML.

The journey culminates in Unit 4: Communicating Data Value. A great architect is also a great leader. This final unit strengthens the communication and leadership skills needed to influence change, teaching you how to articulate the limitations of the current ("as-is") data landscape and present a compelling, business-focused narrative for the future ("to-be") architecture, focusing on quality, cost, and scale.

 

🎯Who Should Enroll? Shift from Technical Expert to Strategic Leader

This curriculum is ideal for data professionals at every stage ready to evolve from technical implementers into strategic architectural leaders. This includes Data Engineers and Modelers seeking advancement, Solution and Enterprise Architects aiming to solidify their data design expertise, BI and Analytics Professionals who need architectural context, and IT and Transformation Leaders focused on aligning data systems with business outcomes.

 

✅Why Start This Journey Now?

Modern organizations demand architects who master both technology and business value. By mastering these principles, you will gain skills that directly translate into competitive advantages: you'll improve data quality and governance, learn how to connect data architecture directly to enterprise goals, and gain the credibility needed to influence strategic decisions. Crucially, this course sets the definitive foundation for the planned SAP Certified Data Architect specialization* launching in 2026!

Visit SAP Learning and enroll in the Becoming an SAP Data Architect – Foundational Learning Journey Today! Becoming an SAP Data Architect (Foundational)

entry.title: Meet Joule: Your AI Copilot for SAP Master Data Governance
entry.content:
If there’s one thing we all want more of in our workday, it’s time. What if you could skip the clicks, stop hunting through endless menus, and just ask for the data or task you need right when you need it? That’s exactly what Joule, SAP’s powerful generative AI assistant, brings to the table. Today, we’ll explore Joule’s capabilities, then zoom in on how it takes SAP Master Data Governance (MDG) to a brand-new level of usability and efficiency.

What is Joule?

Joule is SAP’s generative AI assistant, designed to help you interact with your SAP systems in the simplest way: conversation. Whether you need business information, want to complete a task, or are searching for a specific insight, just ask Joule in plain language. Joule can retrieve information, complete routine tasks, guide you to the right application, or even explore your business data with you, all within the familiar SAP ecosystem.

You can work with Joule in multiple conversation threads, review or delete expired conversations, and move seamlessly between ongoing dialogues. Its capabilities are broad, spanning transactional, navigational, informational, and analytical scenarios.

 

Why Joule?

Joule is not just about simplifying tasks; it is about connecting everyone in your company, especially business users, with professional Master Data Governance (MDG) capabilities. This means that even users without extensive knowledge of MDG applications can accelerate business processes with ease. By removing the need to build custom user interfaces, Joule lowers total cost of ownership, reduces the resources needed to manage MDG, and streamlines communication by automating routine governance tasks. Here are some of the key business benefits Joule brings to your organization:

Enhanced Accessibility and Ease of Use
With Joule, access to core MDG features is democratized. Employees from any department can seamlessly engage in data governance tasks no matter where they are, eliminating the heavy reliance on specialized MDG experts. This opens up data quality and integrity efforts to a broader team and fosters a more inclusive data culture.

Efficiency in Business Processes
Joule automates routine tasks and provides intuitive conversational interfaces for complex operations. As a result, business processes move faster, and everyone spends less time managing data, leading to a significant boost in productivity.

Cost Reduction and TCO Savings
Because Joule delivers built-in, user-friendly interfaces out of the box, there is no need to invest in developing or supporting custom UIs. This helps companies save on development costs and ongoing maintenance, making the solution more cost-effective.

Improved Data Quality
By leveraging large language models to identify and correct errors, Joule helps catch anomalies and typos at the point of entry. This results in better, more consistent data from the start, supporting confident decision-making throughout the business.

Enhanced User Experience
The conversational, intuitive design of Joule makes working with complex data simpler and more enjoyable. Employees can interact with the system naturally, leading to higher satisfaction, stronger engagement, and increased productivity across the company.

Implementing Joule in your MDG landscape brings tangible business value by improving accessibility, accelerating processes, cutting costs, enhancing data quality, and ensuring scalable data management as your organization grows.

Joule for Master Data Governance: A New Era of Productivity

To  help you understand what does this base MDG Joule AI Feature in SAP Business AI Catalog offers, let’s dive into what makes Joule a game-changer for Master Data Governance.

With SAP Master Data Governance on S/4HANA 2023 FSP0 Private Cloud, customers can use following Joule capabilities to enable their business users:

Effortlessly Search and Display Business Partners

Searching for Business Partners is smarter now. You can search by almost any attribute, such as name, address, bank details, tax ID, or even birthdate:

  • “Find all Business Partners in Munich.”
  • “Search BPs with IBAN 789789.”

Joule provides results instantly and can guide you to detailed views or relevant applications for further action.

Display Business Partners

Need to look up a specific Business Partner’s information? Just type:

  • “Show me the bank details for BP 1234567.”
  • “Display details for Business Partner 345678.”

 

Joule can retrieve overviews, basic details, bank accounts, identification numbers, address usages, payment cards, roles, contact relationships, and more. You don’t need to know in advance where in the system this data resides, Joule brings it to you. Once you have the information that you need, you can open the Business Partner record from the Joule chat window or continue working on the record through Joule.

Create Business Partners  (cloud-ready mode)

Creating or updating Business Partners has never been easier. Instead of navigating multiple screens, you can tell Joule what you need in natural language:

  • “Create a new organization named ABC Corp with address in Berlin.”
  • “Create a new person.” (and let Joule walk you through the steps)

Joule guides you through supplying only the necessary details, checks for duplicates, and creates the governance process. Of course, you can always choose to submit, retype, or decline the process—all within your conversational flow.

Edit Business Partners (cloud-ready mode) - On the Fly 

Need to update a detail? No need to open multiple transactions:

  • “Change the organization name for BP 1234567.”
  • “Change the email to example@example.com for partner 4711.”

Joule manages the governance workflow for these changes, letting you save drafts, submit them for approval or even cancel as needed.

Query MDG Process (cloud-ready mode)

Joule isn’t stopping there. With S/4HANA 2025 Private Cloud, managing your governance processes just became much more intuitive:

Now, you can search not just for Business Partners, but for the governance processes themselves - across Central Governance, Consolidation, and Data Quality areas. Need to find a process by the process ID, who created it, its status, or when it was created? Just ask:

  • “Show my last process.”
  • “Find high priority processes from last week.”
  • “Display all processes created by John.”

Once you find a process, Joule brings you the essential details: process ID, status, priority, and more. Need to understand what’s going on with a particular record? Request:

  • “Show me the details of process 1234.”

And from there, you can jump straight to related Business Partner data or the relevant management app.

Bringing It Together

Joule is redefining how we interact with Master Data Governance in SAP. With intuitive conversational access, you can create, search, display, and edit Business Partners or even manage governance processes - without losing your flow or context. No more clicking through menus or remembering transaction codes. You get instant, accurate responses, all backed by the power and security of SAP.

Ready to work smarter? Meet Joule, and experience next-level productivity in Master Data Governance. Take a moment to also check our video on the newest Joule capabilities in MDG with demos and examples.

Interested in learning more about Joule’s capabilities or how to enable it for your team? Reach out to your SAP contact or check out the SAP Help Portal for the latest documentation and setup guides.

entry.title: Hidden Crisis: Ageing Infrastructure, Reactive O&M, and the Data Problem No One Is Solving
entry.content:
Why ageing infrastructure and reactive maintenance are accelerating into a classic hockey-stick cost curve for oil and gas industry, and how trusted master data is the only realistic path to predictive excellence.

Dr. Imad Syed, CEO, PiLog Group 

The oil and gas (O&G) industry remains one of the world’s most critical sources of primary energy, shaping global economic stability and long-term development. Yet behind this strategic importance lies an accelerating challenge: an ageing infrastructure that is pushing operations and maintenance (O&M) into a reactive, high-cost spiral.

Many upstream and midstream assets – platforms, pipelines, processing units and refineries – are now 20-30 years old. Science journal, Nature, found that many of the world’s 12,000 offshore platforms are nearing their end-of-life. More than 3,000 installations in the North Sea and Gulf of Mexico have already exceeded 25-30 years of operation.

Ageing assets drive a predictable but costly pattern: the “hockey-stick” curve of O&M costs. As corrosion, fouling, fatigue, and obsolete parts accumulate, operators are forced into more inspections, more emergency work, more reverse-engineering, and ultimately more unplanned shutdowns. Downtime alone can exceed $1 million per day per asset. Global outages contribute to more than 10 million barrels per day of lost production – representing nearly $200 billion in annual revenue foregone.

Our analysis of 18 global publicly listed O&G companies reveals that O&M already represents 18% of their total operating costs, rising to as high as 35% for some players. This cost pressure is magnified whenever price cycles trigger deferred maintenance, compounding future reliability risk.

 Why Digital Investments Alone Don’t Solve the Problem

The industry has responded with aggressive investments in digital technologies. Global O&G digital transformation spend reached an estimated $77-90 billion in 2024 and is projected to rise to $90-124 billion in 2025: approximately 10-15% of upstream/downstream CAPEX.

Some leaders, including ExxonMobil, Chevron, and Saudi Aramco report strong returns through reduced downtime and optimized assets. However, scaling remains the industry’s biggest weakness: only 13-36% of firms have taken successful pilots into full-scale operations. Nearly 60-70% remain stuck in pilot mode.

The root cause is clear:

Digital tools generate data, but most O&G operators lack the foundational master data integrity required to turn that data into reliable decisions.

Sensors proliferate, but only 13% of companies use this data strategically. Legacy systems Maximo, ERP, SCADA, CMMS, engineering repositories, procurement databases hold conflicting records of the same assets, parts, BOMs, locations, and vendors. Without a ‘single version of the truth’, predictive models cannot be trusted, digital twins cannot remain accurate, and planners spend more time reconciling than optimizing.

The result: fragmented data, stalled initiatives, and a widening gap between digital ambition and operational reality.

Master Data Management (MDM): The Missing Foundation for O&M Transformation

Master Data Management is not a “data-cleaning exercise”,  it is a structural capability that enables oil & gas operators to control the lifecycle, quality, and reliability of asset, equipment, material, and vendor data across all systems. When done properly, it directly addresses both the ageing infrastructure challenge and the industry’s deeply reactive maintenance culture.

MDM gives operators:

  • A single asset golden record (industry-specific) that integrates engineering, EAM, CMMS, inspection, procurement, and sensor data
  • Clean, enriched, audit-ready records of equipment, parts, hierarchies, and BOMs
  • Governance workflows that ensure every change is approved, traceable, and compliant
  • The ability to feed digital twins and AI engines with trusted, high-quality data
  • The foundation required to shift from reactive to predictive maintenance                                                                            

Strengthening Asset Integrity for Ageing Infrastructure

Integrity management depends on reliable equipment history, correct system hierarchies, validated inspection data, and traceable change records. Ageing assets amplify the consequences of poor data – small inaccuracies can lead to missed integrity threats and unplanned shutdowns.

MDM provides:

  • A unified asset master data record (tag, serial, function, location, BOM, history, specs, certificates, maint. plan, etc)
  • Cleansing and enrichment with OEM data, calibration records, and criticality ratings
  • Governance that ensures every change, a modification, retrofit, or decommissioning is documented and approved

The result is an asset register that becomes the single, authoritative source for risk-based integrity planning and regulatory compliance.

 Moving Beyond Reactive Maintenance to Predictive Excellence

Predictive maintenance cannot succeed without clean, consistent, and connected master data. AI/ML models rely on correct sensor-to-asset mapping, harmonized failure codes, and trustworthy historical labels.

MDM enables:

  • Accurate “sensor → asset → system” mapping
  • High-quality historical maintenance data aligned to standard failure modes
  • Enriched material masters with lead times, alternate parts, and criticality
  • Validated data flows into PdM engines, digital twins, and planning systems

Industry estimates indicate that predictive-maintenance excellence made possible by reliable master data can reduce unplanned downtime by 20-30% and cut maintenance costs by 5-15%, rising to 20-30% with advanced AI-based scheduling and spares optimization.

 Reducing Costs Through Better Materials, Spares, and Inventory Data

Good master data creates quantifiable value:

  • Lower inventory and working-capital costs
  • Fewer emergency purchases
  • Higher first-time fix rates
  • Better compliance and reporting
  • Faster work-order planning and execution

O&G companies frequently carry 10-25% duplicate materials, which directly inflate procurement and storage costs. Clean, standardized material masters eliminate duplication, enforce catalogue discipline, and drive more strategic sourcing.

Case 1: The Real Cost of Bad Master Data in Oil & Gas / Petrochemicals

Symptom

Typical Annual Cost (per site)

Source

Duplicate / obsolete spares

$5–25 M

Solomon, Accenture, McKinsey

Excess inventory carrying cost (25–35 %)

$3–12 M

Deloitte 2024

Emergency purchases & expediting fees

$2–8 M

ARC Advisory

Unplanned downtime from “wrong part”

0.5–3 % of revenue (~$10–60 M for large refinery/upstream asset)

Solomon RAM Studies

Contractor inefficiency (re-work, waiting, repeated mobilizations)

15–40 % premium

EY, Bain

Total hidden cost: easily $20–80 M per year for a large refinery or offshore platform.

Case 2: Three Areas Where Clean Master Data Delivers Immediate ROI

  1. Increasing Maintenance Budget (without asking for more money)
    • Clean equipment hierarchies + accurate BOMs → 20–40 % reduction in emergency work orders
    • Accurate failure codes and catalog profiles → better MTBF data → defensible reliability projects
    • Result: You re-allocate 10–25 % of existing maintenance spend from reactive to preventive/predictive → effectively “creates” new budget
  2. Optimizing Spares Inventory (freeing up cash or hording cost)
    • De-duplication projects typically remove 12–15 % of Spares
    • Accurate min-max driven by real consumption (not guesswork) → 20–30 % inventory reduction in 12–18 months
    • Standardized material descriptions → vendor consolidation → source of supply mapping → 8–15 % purchase price reduction
    • Real case: Middle East refinery freed $38 M working capital in 14 months after master data cleanse
  3. Optimizing Inbound Services & Contractor Spend
    • Clean functional locations + Clean equipment classification + standardized task lists → standardized BOMs → work packs issued with 95 %+ material availability
    • Accurate service master and vendor master (service provider) → automated 3-way match → 98 % touchless invoicing
    • Result: 25–40 % reduction in contractor waiting time and repeated mobilizations
    • Example: North Sea operator cut turnaround contractor cost by 18 % in 2024 just by fixing material master and BOMs

How PiLog’s DQG Suite Delivers These Outcomes

Together with SAP, PiLog has embarked on a partnership journey to address these industry challenges.  Complementing and enriching SAP Intelligent Asset Management, PiLog’s Data Quality and Governance (DQG) Suite is built to deliver the exact MDM capabilities required for aging infrastructures, predictive maintenance, and integrated O&M operations. The solution is Endorsed and Premium Certified with over 200+ real-time integrations ready to deploy on-premise, private cloud & SaaS public cloud for SAP Cloud ERP Private & Public editions.

The suite provides:

  • End-to-end data migration, standardization, cleansing, enrichment, and governance
  • AI-enabled classification based on ISO 81346 and ISO 14224
  • Harmonized assets, equipment, and material records for SAP EAM, APM, and S/4HANA
  • Seamless integration with SAP APM, FSM, IBP (MRO), BNAC & Ariba
  • A foundation for digital twins, predictive maintenance, and data-driven operational excellence

Common Pain Points Identified Across PiLog’s O&G Customers

  • Duplicate or inconsistent material masters
  • Unstructured PR/PO creation and poor service masters
  • Missing or incomplete asset and equipment masters
  • Multi-entity inconsistencies across business groups

Quantifiable Improvements Delivered

  • Up to 25% duplicate detection and elimination
  • Up to 15% reduction in inventory hoarding
  • 12% YoY reduction in O&M costs
  • 22%+ reduction in sourcing and procurement cost

These results demonstrate that master data is not an IT function, it is an operational performance driver.

 Aging Assets Are Inevitable – Reactive O&M Is Not

The industry cannot stop infrastructure from ageing. But it can change how it responds.

Digital investments alone will not close the reliability gap. A foundational shift to high-quality master data, governed, enriched, standardized, and connected across all systems – is what allows O&G companies to control the cost curve, extend asset life, and move decisively toward predictive maintenance excellence.

With the right master data foundations, operators can break the cycle of reactive firefighting and build a more reliable, safer, and economically resilient O&M model.

* References can be provided upon request

entry.title: 🚀 Ignite Your Data Architecture Journey: Empower Enterprise and Data Architects Today!
entry.content:
In the era of digital transformation, data is the heartbeat of innovation, strategy, and impactful decision-making. Strong Data Architecture is the foundation that powers every thriving enterprise. To bridge technical expertise with strategic business outcomes, the SAP Data Architecture experts alongside the SIX learning team proudly unveil the Data Architect Learning Journey (Foundational).

This inaugural phase of the Becoming a Data Architect program is crafted to equip aspiring architects, data professionals, and solution experts with essential frameworks, modern methodologies, and core principles that shape successful enterprise data architectures.

 

🏗️ Layered Learning: From Fundamentals to Enterprise Impact

This journey guides you through a carefully designed progression to build your expertise methodically:

  • Unit 1: Foundational Concepts anchors you in the fundamentals—mastering modeling techniques, metadata management, and the integration of data architectures with enterprise frameworks such as Zachman and TOGAF. Grasp the essentials of Conceptual, Logical, and Physical models that form the backbone of data design.

  • Unit 2: Modern Paradigms & Integration immerses you in today’s evolving landscape, exploring advanced patterns like Data Mesh, Data Fabric, Data Lake, and Data Lakehouse. Understand when and how to apply each, plus gain insights into key integration frameworks, including the nuances between ETL and ELT strategies.

  • Unit 3: Governance, Security & Strategic Value addresses the growing need to establish trust and compliance within expansive data ecosystems. Delve into comprehensive data governance frameworks—data stewardship, lineage, and privacy safeguards that secure modern architectures and enable AI/ML innovations.

  • Unit 4: Communicating Data Value sharpens your leadership voice, guiding you to communicate complex architectural changes clearly and compellingly. Learn to narrate the journey from the current state to future architecture, emphasizing quality, cost-efficiency, and scalability to align with business priorities.

 

🎯Who Should Embark?

Designed for data professionals ready to evolve beyond technical roles into influential architectural strategists, this foundation suits:

  • Data Engineers and Modelers aspiring to architect-level roles

  • Solution and Enterprise Architects deepening their data design acumen

  • BI and Analytics experts seeking architectural context

  • IT and Transformation leaders aligning data strategies with business goals

 

✅Why Now? Gain a Strategic Edge

Today’s enterprises demand architects who not only understand technology but who can also drive meaningful business outcomes. This journey empowers you to enhance data quality, governance, and alignment with enterprise strategies—skills that translate directly into leadership credibility and competitive advantage. Plus, it sets the stage for the upcoming SAP Certified Data Architect specialization, launching in 2026.

 

🛣️ Begin Your Path to Architecting Tomorrow

Ready to elevate your data architectural mindset and lead transformative initiatives? No prerequisites required—start your journey today!

Enroll now at SAP Learning: Becoming an SAP Data Architect – Foundational Learning Journey!

entry.title: Recommendation/Best Practice for SAP Master Data Governance Extensibility in Classic Mode
entry.content:
Introduction to Clean Core in S/4HANA

With the introduction of MDG Cloud Ready Mode in SAP S/4HANA 2023, SAP has significantly intensified its emphasis on maintaining a clean core. SAP’s objective is clear: Organizations must transition toward a clean core, a system landscape where customizations are isolated from the core application, ensuring long-term stability, predictable upgrades, and seamless adoption of innovations delivered by SAP.

Customers opting for Cloud Ready Mode encounter stricter guardrails around extensibility. Traditional enhancement patterns—such as modifications, implicit enhancements, or pervasive use of custom code within standard classes—are heavily restricted. Only those extension approaches that guarantee lifecycle stability and upgrade resilience are supported.

Implications for MDG Customers in Classic Mode

For organizations currently using MDG in Classic Mode, but intending to maintain system stability and ensure smooth, low-effort upgrades, it becomes essential to adopt an extension strategy that aligns closely with clean-core guidelines. The extension approach must strike a balance between functional requirements and long-term maintainability, particularly in upgrade-intensive environments.

Organizations in Classic Mode face pressing questions:

  • How do we continue enhancing MDG without jeopardizing upgrade stability?
  • Which extensibility options remain viable, and which should be avoided?
  • How can we design custom logic that aligns with SAP’s forward-looking roadmap?
  • What development patterns minimize rework in future upgrades?

Recommended Extensibility Approach

Following SAP’s clean-core philosophy, the recommended extensibility hierarchy is as follows:

1. Key User In-App Extensibility

Preferred for UI and lightweight business logic enhancements, designed to be upgrade-safe and lifecycle-stable

2. Side-by-Side Extensibility

Ideal for more complex or custom business processes using SAP BTP, providing isolation from the S/4HANA digital core while ensuring deep integration through released APIs and events

3. Classic Extensibility

Classic extensions remain available in MDG Classic Mode, but they should be used sparingly and only when clean-core options cannot fulfil the requirement.

Classic extensibility includes:

  • BADIs specific to MDG
  • Enhancement points
  • Derivation and validation exits
  • Custom code within allowed enhancement frameworks

Considerations for MDG Extension Design

There have been attempts in Classic Mode to build wrapper classes around SAP standard MDG classes with the intention of stabilizing custom logic across upgrades. However, given the expanding set of released APIs and whitelisted objects, attempting to wrap all MDG-specific classes is time-consuming.

A more sustainable approach is to rely on:

  • Released MDG APIs, frameworks, and BAdIs
  • Workflow and rule-based configurability
  • Event-driven extension patterns via SAP BTP
  • Governance processes aligned with clean-core principles

 

Below table will outline the recommended and prioritized extension options for MDG in Classic Mode, offering guidance for customers seeking to preserve upgrade stability while aligning with SAP’s recommended enhancement strategy.

We will continue to revise and update the above list, as and when new scenarios and capabilities are identified.

entry.title: 4 Important enhancements related to Object type in SAP PM
entry.content:
Here are the top four important enhancements related to object type in SAP PM:

  1. Default value of authorization group: The object type can be proposed for motors, pumps, compressors, electrical distribution lines, transmission lines, assembly lines, etc. Now for each of the object types there can be a default value of authorization group. We can maintain those values in a Z table. If any new object type is introduced, a default value can be added. The user can add the respective value of the authorization group later on.
  2. Default value of Planner group: Planner groups generally work for a specific department or specific type of machine. In such a situation we can determine a specific planner group based on object type. This can be done by enhancing and maintaining the planner group and object type in the Z table.
  3. Determine the catalog profile based on the type of object type: The catalog profile is very important for adding technical findings in the form of codes in a notification. The catalog profile should be specific to the technical object or technical object type. So, you can maintain a catalog profile and object type in a Z table and determine the catalog profile automatically at the time of creating a technical object. At the time of creating a notification, the catalog profile is determined automatically as a part of the standard SAP feature.
  4. Determine the class and assign it to the equipment: You can determine the class automatically with respect to object type. For example, for object type "electrical pole," you can do an enhancement to determine its respective class automatically. You need to maintain the class and object type in a z table. It helps to assign the correct class to the technical object.

 

You can use user exit IEQM0003 for the check at the time of saving the equipment.

You can use the BAPI => BAPI_OBJCL_CHANGE to assign the class as per the object type in a equipment.

Regards,

Vaibhav Mishra

entry.title: Rule Mining in a Nutshell, Manage your rules with Master Data Quality Rule Mining
entry.content:
Introduction 

Rule mining, a fundamental element of master data quality management, enables more consistent and systematic data control. It also helps us define new rules, which in turn make data control easier and more organized. As a result, organizations strengthen data integrity, ensure consistency and build a more solid foundation for their business strategy.


Figure 1: MDQ Rule Mining entry point in Fiori 

Why Rule Mining Matters 

 Rule Mining uncovers relationships in your data and makes rule management easier: 

  • Discover new rules: As you can see in the screenshot below, new rules are presented, offering us many options to gain more control over our data.


Figure 2: Recommended Process Rules - System-generated rules based on existing data patterns.

  • Increase efficiency: With the new rules, you can improve data consistency more easily and quickly.


Figure 3: Validation Rules for Rule Mining 

  • Enhance consistency: By performing evaluations and comparisons, you can manage your data more quickly and easily.


Figure 4: Evaluate Data Quality  

  • Simplify maintenance: You can update BRF+ tables directly via Fiori without manually entering BRF+ information.


Figure 5: Simplified BRF+ Workbench 

Business Benefits 

Rule Mining is not just a technical feature—it directly adds value to business processes: 

  • Better data quality: Rules enforce completeness and accuracy. 
  • Operational speed: Clean data accelerates processes such as onboarding, procurement, and reporting. 
  • Strategic advantage: Reliable data supports stronger decision-making. 
  • Time savings: You can save time by managing rules more quickly.

Conclusion 

MDQ Rule Mining allows you to discover and apply rules directly from your data, achieving efficiency and consistency. Using this method, we both improved data quality and discovered the ability to easily update BRF+ tables from Fiori, leading to faster rule management solutions than ever before. You too can improve data quality and save time by discovering new rules faster.

Thank you for reading,

Best regards.

Ahmet

entry.title: Replicating DMS Documents via Web Services in MDG Scenarios
entry.content:
Content

  • Content
  • 1. Introduction
  • 2. Scenario and Feasible Option
  • 3. Technical Implementation
  • 3.a Architecture Overview
  • 3.b Service Activation and Connection Setup
  • Service Activation
  • SM59 HTTP Destinations
  • 3.c Class Structure
  • 3.d Read Document Links
  • 3.e Download Files via OData
  • CSRF Token Handling
  • Retrieving Attachment Metadata
  • Downloading Binary Content
  • 3.f Create Document in Target System
  • DIR Creation
  • Object Link Creation
  • File Upload
  • 3.g Hook into MDG Outbound Processing
  • 4. Conclusion

 

1. Introduction

In SAP MDG scenarios, master data is governed centrally and replicated to connected systems using web services via the Data Replication Framework (DRF). For Business Partner data, this covers core attributes such as general data, roles, addresses, and bank details.

In many implementations, suppliers and customers have documents attached using SAP Document Management System (DMS). These documents are stored as Document Info Records (DIR) and linked to business objects via table DRAD. During Business Partner replication, document references are transferred as part of the message structure.

However, the content of the original files is not transferred. When source and target systems use different content servers, this results in document links in the target system that point to files users cannot access. Standard options such as DOCMAS IDocs are not web service–based and are not integrated with DRF, and SAP does not provide a standard solution for transferring file content in such scenarios.

This blog presents a custom approach that uses standard OData APIs to download document content from the source system and recreate Document Info Records with attachments in the target system, integrated into the MDG outbound replication process.

Note: This solution targets on-premise SAP environments. SAP S/4HANA Cloud offers its own DMS capabilities, which are outside the scope of this blog.

 

2. Scenario and Feasible Option

In our scenario, DMS documents are linked to ERP vendors in the MDG hub system. When vendors are replicated to target systems via web service, the document links are transferred but the actual files are not. Since the systems do not share a common DMS server, users in the target system receive document references that point to files they cannot access.

Two fundamental gaps prevent standard functionality from addressing this requirement: no web service-oriented document replication method exists, and file content cannot be transferred between separate content servers. A custom solution is required. The proposed approach consists of three steps:

  1. Read document links from the source system: Query the DRAD table to identify all DMS documents linked to the vendor being replicated.
  2. Download files from the source system: Use API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV to retrieve the actual file content for each linked document.
  3. Create documents in the target system: Use API_DMS_PROCESS_SRV to create the Document Info Record and API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV to upload the file content. Then establish the object link to the replicated vendor.

This approach runs within the MDG outbound processing framework, ensuring that document replication is triggered automatically whenever a vendor is replicated via web services.

 

3. Technical Implementation

3.a Architecture Overview


Figure 1: DMS Document replication architecture overview

3.b Service Activation and Connection Setup

Before implementing the replication logic, the required OData services must be activated and the HTTP connections must be configured.

Service Activation

OData services must be activated in both systems via t-code /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE:

  • API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV – API for Attachment Service
  • API_DMS_PROCESS_SRV – API for DMS

SM59 HTTP Destinations

In the source system, two HTTP destinations must be created in transaction SM59 to connect to the target system. Both destinations use connection type H (HTTP Connection to ABAP System).

Z_MER_DMS – Points to the API_DMS_PROCESS_SRV service. This destination is used for creating Document Info Records and establishing object links to the supplier.
Z_MER_DOCUMENT – Points to the API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV service. This destination is used for uploading file content to the DIR in the target system.


Figure 2: Configuration for Z_MER_DMS destination
Figure 3: Configuration for Z_MER_DOCUMENT

Both destinations should be configured with appropriate authentication credentials for the target system.

3.c Class Structure

The replication logic is encapsulated in a custom class. This class is initialized with a configuration structure and provides a single public method EXECUTE to trigger the replication process. Internally, the class handles reading document links, downloading files, and creating documents in the target system through private methods.


Figure 4: Replication class methods

The constructor accepts a configuration structure that controls the replication behavior:

TYPES: 
  BEGIN OF ty_config,
    source_supplier   TYPE lifnr, "Supplier number in source system
    target_supplier   TYPE lifnr, "Supplier number in target system
    target_doc_type   TYPE dokar, "Document type to be used in target system
    storage_category  TYPE string, "Content server storage category
    cv_destination    TYPE c LENGTH 20, "Destination name for attachment API
    dms_destination   TYPE c LENGTH 20, "Destination name for DMS API
  END OF ty_config.

 

3.d Read Document Links

The first step identifies all DMS documents linked to the supplier being replicated. This information is stored in the DRAD table, which maintains object links between DIR and business objects.

SELECT DISTINCT dokar, doknr, dokvr, doktl
  FROM drad
  INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE _document_keys
  WHERE objky = _supplier AND dokob = 'LFA1'.

 

3.e Download Files via OData

Once the document links are identified, the actual files are downloaded from the source system using API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV. This process involves two calls: first to retrieve attachment metadata, then to download the binary content.

CSRF Token Handling

Before making any OData calls, a CSRF token must be fetched:

lv_url = gc_cv_service_path && '/$metadata'.

cl_http_utility=>set_request_uri( request = lo_http_client->request  uri = lv_url ).

lo_http_client->request->set_header_field( name = 'x-csrf-token' value = 'Fetch' ).
lo_http_client->request->set_method( 'GET' ).

lo_http_client->send( ).
lo_http_client->receive( ).

lv_csrf_token = lo_http_client->response->get_header_field( 'x-csrf-token' ).

Retrieving Attachment Metadata

For each DIR key, the attachment metadata is retrieved through the navigation property:

lv_url = gc_cv_service_path &&
         |/A_DocumentInfoRecordAttch(| &&
         |DocumentInfoRecordDocType='{ ls_dir_key-dokar }',| &&
         |DocumentInfoRecordDocNumber='{ ls_dir_key-doknr }',| &&
         |DocumentInfoRecordDocVersion='{ ls_dir_key-dokvr }',| &&
         |DocumentInfoRecordDocPart='{ ls_dir_key-doktl }')
         /DocumentInfoRecordToAttachmentNavigation|.

This returns metadata including LogicalDocument, ArchiveDocID, Filename, and MimeType.

Downloading Binary Content

The actual file content is downloaded using the $value endpoint:

lv_url = gc_cv_service_path &&
         |/AttachmentContentSet(| &&
         |DocumentInfoRecordDocType='{ is_nav-documentinforecorddoctype }',| &&
         |DocumentInfoRecordDocNumber='{ is_nav-documentinforecorddocnumber }',| &&
         |LogicalDocument='{ is_nav-logicaldocument }',| &&
         |ArchiveDocumentID='{ is_nav-archivedocumentid }',| &&
         |...)/$value|.

lo_http_client->send( ).
lo_http_client->receive( ).

rs_file-content = lo_http_client->response->get_data( ).  " Returns xstring

The get_data( ) method returns the binary content as xstring.

3.f Create Document in Target System

Creating a document in the target system involves three sub-steps: creating the Document Info Record, establishing the object link to the supplier, and uploading the file content.

DIR Creation

First, a new Document Info Record is created via API_DMS_PROCESS_SRV:

lv_url = '/A_DocumentInfoRecord'.
lv_json = |\{| &&  |"DocumentInfoRecordDocType":"{ ms_config-target_doc_type }",| &&
          |"DocumentInfoRecordDocVersion":"00",| &&
          |"DocumentInfoRecordDocPart":"000"| &&  |\}|.

io_dms_client->request->set_method( 'POST' ).
io_dms_client->request->set_header_field( name = 'x-csrf-token' value = iv_csrf_token ).
io_dms_client->request->set_header_field( name = 'Content-Type' value = 'application/json' ).
io_dms_client->request->set_cdata( lv_json ).

Object Link Creation

After the DIR is created, the link to the supplier is established via the to_ProductObj navigation property:

lv_url = |/A_DocumentInfoRecord(| &&
         |DocumentInfoRecordDocType='{ is_dir_key-dokar }',| &&
         |DocumentInfoRecordDocNumber='{ is_dir_key-doknr }',| &&
         |DocumentInfoRecordDocVersion='{ is_dir_key-dokvr }',| &&
         |DocumentInfoRecordDocPart='{ is_dir_key-doktl }'/to_ProductObj|.

lv_json = |\{| && |"LinkedSAPObject":"LFA1",| &&
          |"LinkedSAPObjectKey":"{ lv_supplier }"| &&  |\}|.

File Upload

Finally, the file content is uploaded via API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV. The filename is passed in the header and the binary content is sent in the request body:

lv_url = |/A_DocumentInfoRecordAttch(| &&
         |DocumentInfoRecordDocType='{ is_dir_key-dokar }',| &&
         |DocumentInfoRecordDocNumber='{ is_dir_key-doknr }',| &&
         |DocumentInfoRecordDocVersion='{ is_dir_key-dokvr }',| &&
         |DocumentInfoRecordDocPart='{ is_dir_key-doktl }')
         /DocumentInfoRecordToAttachmentNavigation|.

io_cv_client->request->set_method( 'POST' ).
io_cv_client->request->set_header_field( name = 'x-csrf-token' value = iv_csrf_token ).
io_cv_client->request->set_header_field( name = 'Content-Type' 
                                  value = 'application/octet-stream' ).
io_cv_client->request->set_header_field( name = 'Slug' value = is_file-filename ).
io_cv_client->request->set_header_field( name = 'StorageCategory' 
                                         value = ms_config-storage_category ).
io_cv_client->request->set_data( is_file-content ).

 

3.g Hook into MDG Outbound Processing

To trigger document replication automatically during Business Partner replication, we implement the IF_MDG_SE_BP_BULK_REPLRQ_OUT interface.

METHOD if_mdg_se_bp_bulk_replrq_out~outbound_processing.

  DATA(lv_bp_number) = in-partner-header-object_instance-bpartner.
  
  IF lv_bp_number IS INITIAL.  
   RETURN.  
  ENDIF.

  DATA(lo_replicator) = NEW zcl_dms_doc_replication(
    is_config = VALUE #(
      source_supplier  = lv_bp_number
      target_supplier  = lv_bp_number  "Generally same with source supplier number
      cv_destination   = 'Z_MER_DOCUMENT'
      dms_destination  = 'Z_MER_DMS'
      target_doc_type  = 'KPR'
      storage_category = 'DMS_C1_ST' ) ).

  TRY.
      DATA(ls_result) = lo_replicator->execute( ).
    CATCH cx_static_check INTO DATA(lx_error).
      " Handle error
  ENDTRY.

ENDMETHOD.

This ensures that whenever a supplier is replicated through the standard MDG replication process, the associated DMS documents are replicated automatically.

 

4. Conclusion

In web service-based SAP MDG scenarios, replicating Business Partner master data without its associated DMS documents leads to incomplete and confusing results for end users. This blog demonstrated a practical and reusable approach to closing this gap by leveraging standard OData APIs and integrating the logic directly into the MDG outbound processing framework. By this logic, DMS documents can be replicated automatically whenever a supplier is replicated.

The same concept can be extended to other business objects, such as customers or materials, that use DMS attachments. Until SAP delivers a web service–based document replication solution integrated with DRF, this approach provides a robust alternative for MDG implementations that require consistent document availability across systems.

 

Thank you for reading.
Best regards,

Merve

entry.title: Switch to the new mass apps for business partner master data on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition!
entry.content:
Do you still use the deprecated apps for mass load and mass maintenance for business partners on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition? It is now to benefit from the successor apps and prepare your environment before the old apps will be removed. This post provides you with more information and details on how to make the switch.

⚠️Important This activity must be completed before the deprecated apps are no longer available! This is planned for release 2608.

 

Why should I switch?

The new apps are enhanced in several dimensions:

  • Useability is enhanced with a new dialog to start a mass change process that allows you to more flexibly define the selection criteria. Selection criteria can now be reviewed and refined from the top of the page. It is no longer required to specify a scope of the change. Overall, this makes working with the app more intuitive.
  • Using data from a file for a mass change or mass load is now directly possible from the aforementioned dialog.
  • These 2 changes make the Manage Mass Maintenance app the central point to start mass processes. No need for the separate Tile Start Mass Processing.
  • The new version of the app provides additional process attributes, such as due date.
  • Customers with the additional license for SAP Master Data Governance can now display each business partner in the app Manage Business Partner – Central Governance, providing a single view on the complete business partner (see Additional Scope Items below).
  • Finally, the new version offers the most recent user experience and generic features of SAP Fiori, for example to collaborate with other users

What must I do?

There are 3 basic steps for making the switch happen:

  1. First, you should finalize all ongoing mass maintenance processes or delete the process if it is no longer relevant. This is recommended as processes created with the deprecated version of the Manage Mass Maintenance app are no longer visible in the new version of the app.
  2. Activate the feature Successor MDG apps for business partners (MDC_BP_CLOUD_READY).
  3. Remove the deprecated Business Catalogs Mass Load - Business Partner Master (Deprecated) (SAP_CMD_BC_BP_MASSLOAD_PC) and Mass Maintenance - Business Partner Master (Deprecated) (SAP_CMD_BC_BP_MASSMAINT_PC). Assign the successor Business Catalog Master Data Governance - Mass Maintenance for Business Partners (SAP_CMD_BC_BPCS_MAINT_PC). This will adjust your role to the updated business role template Master Data Specialist - Business Partner Data (SAP_BR_BUPA_MASTER_SPECIALIST).
  4. Finalize the procedure by starting the app Export Master Data - Business Partners (Fiori ID F6551A) and export any selection of business partners (at least one).
    Important: This is a mandatory step that completes technical setting to enable all new apps!

In case you need more detailed instructions, here they are:

More Detailed Instructions

How to activate the feature?

Follow the documentation Activate Features to find the correct configuration environment according to your system landscape. Eventually, you will find the item Basic Settings and launch this item.

This leads you to the activity Activate Features. Search for the feature Successor MDG apps for business partners (MDC_BP_CLOUD_READY) and activate it. Note: The feature is not reversible, so please test your processes before activating in your production tenant!

How to change the business role for my users?

Examine the business role that you use for your master data specialists using the app Maintain Business Roles. On tab Business Catalogs you will find the two catalogs Mass Load - Business Partner Master (Deprecated) (SAP_CMD_BC_BP_MASSLOAD_PC) and Mass Maintenance - Business Partner Master (Deprecated) (SAP_CMD_BC_BP_MASSMAINT_PC). Note that both of them are marked as deprecated.

Remove these catalogs form the business role. Instead, add the catalog Master Data Governance - Mass Maintenance for Business Partners (SAP_CMD_BC_BPCS_MAINT_PC).

You can also use the function Manage Changes After Upgrade that becomes visible once you switch the business role to edit mode.

If your business role is based on the business role template Master Data Specialist - Business Partner Data (SAP_BR_BUPA_MASTER_SPECIALIST), you can also use the app Business Role Templates to compare and adjust the role.

How to finalize with the export?

It is important to finalize the switch procedure by performing an arbitrary selection of business partners, at least one. Launch the app Export Master Data - Business Partners (Fiori ID F6551A). For example, in the section Define Scope enter BUT000 in the field Select Tables, then enter a business partner number that will select at least one business partner. Also make sure that not too many are selected to avoid spending too much time. After that press Export and if you are satisfied with selected number of business partners, once more Export. There is no need to download the file. It is only important that the export completes.

Done!

You should now be able to use the new app Manage Mass Maintenance – BP (Fiori ID F7974) and all the other new apps included in the new business catalog.

Additional Scope Items

Customers with the additional license for SAP Master Data Governance can active scope item Central Governance for Business Partner (1N7) for additional capabilities.

entry.title: Business Partner Master Data in SAP Cloud ERP 2602
entry.content:
In today's fast-paced digital economy, the integrity, security, and accessibility of master data are paramount to operational excellence. High-quality Business Partner data forms the foundation of countless critical processes, from procurement and sales to finance and compliance. I am pleased to share some of the latest innovations within SAP S/4HANA Cloud that are designed to elevate your business partner master data management strategy.

SAP Cloud ERP is part of SAP Business Suite that brings AI, data, and applications together into a fully integrated system. This community is open for anyone in the cloud ERP ecosystem–customers, partners, and SAP experts.  

To strengthen security and data integrity, we implement header-level authorization checks and proactive duplicate detection to prevent unauthorized access and ensure high-quality, reliable data from the outset. At the same time, onward replication across multisystem landscapes and conversational AI through Joule enable seamless synchronization and intuitive access to information, driving efficiency, transparency, and faster decision-making across the enterprise.


This post will provide an overview of four significant new features: 
1. Enhanced security through header-level authorization checks.
2. Proactive data quality management with an API-based duplicate check.
3. Streamlined data synchronization via onward replication in multisystem landscapes.
4. Efficient data access using our conversational AI, Joule.

1. Enable Business Partner Header-Level Authorization Checks Based on Data Controller and Purpose

Data privacy and compliance are non-negotiable. To provide more granular control over sensitive information, we have introduced the ability to enable authorization checks at the business partner header level based on the combination of a Data Controller and a Purpose.

Feature Scope:
This feature allows for the assignment of specific data controller and purpose combinations directly to a business partner record. Subsequently, the system can enforce authorization checks based on these assignments.

Key Benefits:
The primary advantage is a significant enhancement in access control. With this feature activated, a user must be explicitly authorized for the specific data controller and purpose assigned to a business partner to gain access to that record. This ensures that only relevant personnel can view or process sensitive data, strengthening your data governance and compliance posture in line with regulations like GDPR.

 

Business partner: assignment of purposes along with data controllers

2. Business Partner A2X OData API Operation for Duplicate Check

Maintaining a clean and duplicate-free master data repository is a constant challenge, especially in integrated environments. To address this proactively, we have extended the capabilities of our core Business Partner OData service.

Feature Scope:
A new operation for performing a duplicate check has been added to the `API_BUSINESS_PARTNER` A2X OData service. This operation can be invoked for Business Partners, Customers, and Suppliers.

Key Benefits:
This new functionality empowers external applications to identify potential duplicates before a new master data record is created via the API. By embedding this check into the creation process, organizations can prevent the introduction of redundant data at the source. This is a crucial step toward retaining high master data quality, reducing downstream cleanup efforts, and ensuring process consistency across the enterprise.

Please refer to following Blogs:

  • Using Business Partner OData API (Saranga)
  • Deep Payload for Business Partner OData API (Mani)

Duplicate Check in Business Partner A2X

 

3. Business Partner Integration: Onward Replication in a Multisystem Landscape

For organizations managing complex system landscapes, ensuring data consistency across all systems is a major undertaking. Our new onward replication capability simplifies this process significantly.

Feature Scope:
This feature allows you to trigger the onward replication of incoming business partner data from your SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP system to all other connected systems in your landscape. Activation is managed through a simple self-service configuration user interface (SSCUI).

Key Benefits:
This innovation enables your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system to function as a true master data hub. When a business partner is created or changed, the system automatically distributes these updates to all subscribed downstream systems. This automated synchronization ensures data consistency across the entire landscape, reduces manual integration efforts, and minimizes the risk of data-related process failures.

Onward Replication of Business Partner Master Data in SAP S/4HANA Cloud ERP

 

Community Blog: Onward Replication of Business Partner master data in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

4. Joule as Efficient Access to Business Partner, Customer, and Supplier Master Data

In our pursuit of a more intelligent and user-friendly ERP experience, we have integrated our conversational AI, Joule, with master data management.

Feature Scope:
Joule now allows users to efficiently access attributes of Business Partner, Customer, and Supplier master data objects using natural language queries.

Key Benefits:
This feature fundamentally streamlines the process of data retrieval for business users. Instead of navigating through multiple Fiori apps and screens, a user can simply ask Joule a question, such as "Show me the addresses for Business Partner 100345" or "What are the dunning data details for Customer XYZ?". The key benefits include:

- Enhanced Productivity: Immediate access to necessary data saves time and effort, allowing analysts to focus on decision-making rather than data retrieval.
- Reduced UI Clicks: Joule provides extremely quick information about attributes without requiring users to open dedicated applications.
- Intuitive Interaction: Natural language input makes data access more accessible to a wider range of users.
BP, Customer, Supplier Master Data in SAP Joule

Joule can provide details ranging from a business partner overview, roles, and relationships to specific customer sales area data and supplier purchasing organization details.

 


Community Blog; Exploring Business Partner Master Data with SAP Joule, the AI Co-pilot

These four features represent a significant step forward in making master data management more secure, intelligent, and efficient within SAP S/4HANA Cloud. By enhancing security, improving data quality at the point of entry, simplifying landscape-wide synchronization, and accelerating user access, we are empowering our customers to build a more robust and reliable data foundation for their enterprise.

📽️ Watch the replays of our SAP Cloud ERP 2602 Early Release Series! 

From January 12-16, we hosted a series of 22 expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP Cloud ERP 2602 release. Missed the live sessions? We've got you covered! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you. You will find the recording and presentation for LoB/Industry/Topic as well as 21 other topics of interest. Don't miss this chance and watch them on demand–anywhere, anytime. 

🔎 Find all our assets 

We at Cloud ERP and SCM Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition – Maintenance Windows and Major Upgrade Windows in 2026
entry.content:
Major Upgrade Windows

As in 2025, the SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition has 4 Major Upgrade Windows in 2026. These 24 hours of potential system unavailability are used to upgrade the systems to the next major version.

  • May 16th – 17th (Release 2605)
  • August 15th – 16th (Release 2608)
  • November 14th – 15th (Release 2611)

Depending on the data center region, the 24 hours downtime will start/end at:

  • Europe:      SAT   4 am – SUN   4 am UTC
  • Americas: SAT 10 am – SUN 10 am UTC

Weekly Maintenance Windows

Potentially every weekend, the SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition could use a downtime of 4 hours, to apply database updates, Hotfix Collections and other changes.

Depending on the data center region, the 4 hours downtime may start at:

  • Europe:      SAT 9 pm UTC
  • Americas: SUN 5 am UTC

The following events for the release 2602 are planned as Weekly Maintenance Windows in 2026:

  • Hotfix Collection ... for Release 2602 | ... (information will be updated accordingly)

Cloud System Notification Subscriptions (CSNS)

Please register to Cloud System Notification Subscriptions (CSNS) in SAP for Me to get important information about planned and unplanned downtimes:

  • Please register to Cloud System Notification Subscriptions (CSNS) in SAP for Me to get important information about planned and unplanned downtimes:

    • Access to Cloud System Notification Subscriptions (CSNS): https://me.sap.com/systemsprovisioning/getNotified
    • Find more information about Cloud System Notification Subscriptions (CSNS) in this documentation
    • To find SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition entry select:
      • Subscription Type: Cloud Service
      • Solution Area: SAP Master Data Governance, cloud edition

References

  • Maintenance Windows and Major Upgrade Windows for SAP Cloud Services
  • SAP Note 2888562 – Intelligent Enterprise Suite: Harmonized release calendar for SAP Cloud products
  • Service Level Agreement for SAP Cloud Services

entry.title: SAP Data Reconciliation: Moving from Matching Maze to The Autonomous Future
entry.content:
BACKGROUND

There are two types of SAP reconciliation meetings during cutover.

Meeting Type 1 (What we planned):

“Let’s quickly confirm data moved correctly.”

Meeting Type 2 (What actually happens):

  • Finance says revenue looks off.
  • Procurement says suppliers are complaining.
  • Supply chain says inventory is inflated.
  • IT says record counts match.
  • Everyone stares at IT.

Welcome to the beautiful chaos of SAP data reconciliation.

Whether you’re:

  • Splitting a company
  • Running S/4 alongside ECC
  • Sharing data with subsidiaries
  • Migrating to a new instance
  • Or integrating systems after M&A

Data matching in SAP is not just technical data reconciliation. It’s business continuity surgery.

Why SAP Reconciliation Feels So Painful Today with current ETL tools?

Let’s be honest about the current state.

We Reconcile Tables, Not Decisions

We check:

  • Counts
  • Totals
  • Field matches

But business runs on:

  • Commitments
  • Revenue recognition
  • Inventory valuation
  • Working capital

Matching tables ≠ preserving business intent.

SAP Processes Are Chains, Not Records

Every transaction is part of a flow:

  • Forecast → Production → Inventory → Sale → Revenue
  • Contract → Order → Delivery → Billing → Cash
  • Budget → Commitment → Accrual → Payment

Break one link, and financial truth bends quietly.

Reconciliation today doesn’t understand chains. It understands spreadsheets.

Customization Is Everywhere

Every SAP system has:

  • Custom fields
  • Custom pricing logic
  • Custom movement types
  • Custom account determination
  • Custom approval flows

Generic reconciliation tools look at this and say: “Good luck.”

Real Business Reconciliation KPIs (Not Row Counts)

Let’s talk about reconciliation the way your CFO thinks about it.

Procure-to-Pay (P2P): The “Where Did My Money Go?” Flow

Business Flow:

PO Amount → GR Amount → Invoice Amount → Payment Amount

Real Business KPIs:

PO to GR Consumption Ratio

What % of PO value has been physically received?

  • Detects over-delivery or under-delivery
  • Identifies ghost POs
  • Highlights operational leakage
If PO is $1M and GR is $1.4M, someone is shopping.

GR to Invoice Variance %

Are vendors invoicing more than what was received?

  • Detects pricing issues
  • Catches duplicate invoicing
  • Flags freight/tax mismatches

This is not technical reconciliation. This is margin protection.

Order-to-Cash (O2C): The “Where Did My Revenue Go?” Flow

Business Flow:

Sales Order → Delivery → Billing → AR → Cash

Real Business KPIs:

Order Fulfillment Integrity

% of orders fully delivered as priced.

  • Detects partial deliveries not billed
  • Finds pricing condition errors
  • Highlights logistics-billing gaps

Delivery-to-Billing Variance

Does billing reflect delivered quantity and agreed price? This catches:

  • Wrong pricing conditions
  • Missed surcharges
  • Freight miscalculation

Revenue leakage hides here.

Manufacturing: The “Why Is My Warehouse Lying?” Flow

Business Flow:

Material → Production → Inventory → Valuation → COGS

Real Business KPIs:

Inventory Valuation Consistency

Does total inventory value match between systems by:

  • Plant
  • Material group
  • Valuation class

Mismatch here = financial statement distortion.

BOM Integrity Impact

% of production orders where:

  • Component consumption matches BOM
  • Standard cost matches actual cost

Incorrect BOM migration? It is cost explosion.

The Real Question for the Future

When we reshare data between SAP systems in the future, we should not ask:

“Did the data copy and tables and fields match?”

We should ask:

“Did the business outcome stay intact?”

That is a completely different problem. And it requires a completely different capability set.

The Future: Business-First, Agentic SAP Reconciliation

Let’s imagine what reconciliation should look like 5 years from now.

Not reactive. Not 400 dashboards..

But autonomous, business-aware, and proactive.

Flow-Centric Reconciliation

Instead of reconciling:

  • Material master
  • Vendor master
  • GL master

We reconcile:

  • Revenue lifecycle integrity
  • Working capital stability
  • Margin preservation
  • Inventory flow accuracy
  • Commitment-to-cash cycles

Example:

Instead of: “Material record count and fields matches.” The system says:

“Inventory valuation variance of 1.8% driven by missing moving average price adjustments in 3 plants. Financial impact: $2.1M.”

That’s business reconciliation.

Agentic Automation Capabilities of Future Data Reconciliation Tool

Here’s what real agentic SAP reconciliation must be capable of:

1. Business Context Modeling

The system must understand:

  • How revenue is recognized
  • How inventory is valued
  • How pricing conditions affect margin
  • How tax determination impacts compliance
  • How cost flows through production

Not just tables — but intent.

2. Cross-Process Correlation

If:

  • Billing increases
  • But cash conversion drops
  • And AR aging shifts

The system correlates these across modules. SD + FI + CO + Treasury. Today, that takes 3 teams and 2 weeks.

Future system: “Cash conversion delay traced to payment term misalignment for customers migrated from legacy region.”

3. KPI Stability Monitoring

When data is migration between SAP systems, the system should monitor:

  • Revenue per customer
  • Margin per product
  • Inventory turns
  • Working capital ratio
  • DSO / DPO shifts
  • Production yield

If KPIs deviate outside normal behavior bands. Alert triggered. Because business drift matters more than table drift.

4. Intelligent Variance Classification

Not every difference is a problem. Agentic reconciliation should classify:

  • Configuration difference
  • Timing difference
  • Master data gap
  • Transaction sequencing issue
  • True financial error

And suggest action.

Not just: “Mismatch found.”
But: “Difference due to delayed goods issue posting in target system. Expected to auto-correct in next batch.”

That reduces panic and so many meetings.

Executive-Ready Narrative

Future reconciliation shouldn’t produce:

  • 47 dashboards
  • 16 pivot graphs
  • A nervous data analyst

It should produce:

“Post SAP data migration, revenue and margin remain stable within historical variance bands. Minor inventory timing differences detected in 2 plants with no financial impact. No systemic control breaks identified.”

That’s board ready confidence.

The Big Shift: From Data Movement to Business Assurance

Old model:

Move data. Validate data. Sign off.

Future model:

Simulate business impact. Continuously assure process integrity. Autonomously detect drift. Explain root cause.

Reconciliation becomes less about matching numbers…and more about protecting enterprise behavior.

What Organizations Expect in Future SAP Reconciliations?

In the future, companies won’t ask: “How many records mismatched?” They’ll ask:

  • Are warehouse movements as expected?
  • Did margin integrity hold?
  • Did working capital spike?
  • Did revenue timing shift?
  • Did operational velocity slow down?
  • Did risk exposure increase?
  • Does financial demand and supply are working as expected?

That’s reconciliation redefined in the era of agentic automation!!

entry.title: Master Data Readiness: The Foundation for S/4HANA Transformation and AI Success
entry.content:
Why Data Readiness is No Longer Optional

In the rush to modernise ERP landscapes and adopt AI-driven insights, organisations often overlook a fundamental truth: the success of your S/4HANA transformation and AI initiatives is directly proportional to the quality of your master data. Poor data quality doesn't just slow down migration projects, it can derail AI use cases before they even begin and undermine the business value of your entire digital transformation investment.

As SAP Architects, we've seen the pattern repeatedly: organisations invest millions in S/4HANA implementations and AI platforms, only to discover that inconsistent customer records, duplicate vendor entries, and incomplete material master data render their advanced analytics and AI models ineffective. The promise of intelligent automation and predictive insights remains just that a promise when built on a foundation of fragmented, unreliable data.

  • 85% of AI projects fail to deliver promised value (Gartner)
  • $3.1 trillion annually lost due to poor data quality (IBM)
  • Clear link between data quality and AI failure
  • 40% of business initiatives fail due to data quality issues (MIT Sloan Management Review)

The stakes have never been higher. As organisations race to leverage AI for competitive advantage, master data readiness has shifted from a technical necessity to a strategic imperative.

The Master Data Challenge in S/4HANA Transformations

S/4HANA transformations expose master data issues that may have been hidden or tolerated in legacy ECC environments. The simplified data model, real-time processing capabilities, and integrated analytics of S/4HANA demand a level of data quality and consistency that many organisations simply don't have.

Common Master Data Issues We Encounter:

Data Quality Problems:

  • Inconsistent naming conventions across entities (customers, materials, vendors)
  • Incomplete or missing critical attributes
  • Duplicate records with no clear system of record
  • Inaccurate hierarchies and organisational structures
  • Outdated or obsolete data cluttering the system

Data Governance Gaps:

  • Unclear ownership and accountability for data domains
  • Lack of standardised processes for data creation and maintenance
  • No enforcement of data quality rules
  • Multiple systems maintaining conflicting versions of truth
  • Absence of data stewardship roles and responsibilities

Integration Complexity:

  • Master data scattered across SAP and non-SAP systems
  • No unified view of critical business entities
  • Manual reconciliation processes prone to errors
  • Point-to-point integrations creating data silos
  • Legacy systems unable to provide clean data feeds

These challenges don't disappear when you move to S/4HANA they multiply. And when you layer AI initiatives on top of poor-quality data, you're essentially teaching your algorithms to learn from and perpetuate your data problems.

Why Master Data Quality is the Foundation of AI Success

Here's the reality that many organisations discover too late: AI is only as intelligent as the data it learns from. Machine learning models trained on incomplete, inconsistent, or biased data will produce unreliable predictions. Natural language processing applied to unstructured data with poor quality metadata will fail to deliver meaningful insights.

The Cost of Poor Data in AI:

  • AI models that produce unreliable predictions, eroding business trust
  • Bias amplification when training data contains historical data quality issues
  • Inability to achieve expected ROI on AI investments
  • Manual intervention required to correct AI outputs, defeating automation goals
  • Delayed or abandoned AI initiatives due to data readiness gaps
  • Compliance and ethical risks from biased or incomplete training data

The harsh truth: you cannot AI-transform your way out of a data quality problem. Clean, governed, and unified master data is not a nice-to-have it's the prerequisite for AI success.

 

The Hidden Complexity: Unifying SAP and Non-SAP Data

One of the most underestimated challenges in achieving data readiness is the distributed nature of enterprise data. Critical master data doesn't live in SAP alone it's scattered across:

Common Non-SAP Master Data Sources:

CRM Systems, PLM Platforms, E-commerce Platforms, MDM Solutions, Procurement Systems and homegrown Applications to name a few.

 

The Integration Challenge

For S/4HANA transformations, this distributed landscape creates significant complexity:

  • Multiple versions of truth for the same business entity
  • No single system owns the golden record
  • Manual reconciliation required before migration
  • Risk of migrating conflicting or duplicate data
  • Difficulty tracing data lineage and transformation logic

For AI initiatives, the challenge is even more acute:

  • AI models require comprehensive data across all systems
  • Training data must represent the complete business context
  • Incomplete data leads to biased or unreliable models
  • Real-time AI use cases need synchronised data across systems
  • Data scientists spend most of their time on data preparation instead of model development
  •  

Building the Foundation: A Unified Approach to Master Data

The solution requires a unified master data strategy that brings together SAP and non-SAP data into a single, governed, and trusted source of truth. This foundation serves both your S/4HANA transformation and your AI journey.

Key Components of a Unified Master Data Strategy:

  1. 1. Centralised Master Data Management

Establish a central hub for managing critical business entities:

  • Single authoritative source for customers, products, suppliers, and financial master data
  • Unified data model that spans SAP and non-SAP systems
  • Golden record creation through intelligent matching and merging
  • Real-time synchronisation to downstream systems
  • API-first architecture for AI and analytics consumption
  1. Data Quality and Governance Framework

Implement systematic data quality management:

  • Automated data quality rules and validation checks
  • Duplicate detection and resolution workflows
  • Data standardisation and enrichment processes
  • Workflow-driven governance with clear ownership
  • Continuous monitoring and quality KPIs
  • Data stewardship programs with accountability

Common Pitfalls and How to Avoid Them

Pitfall 1: Underestimating Data Cleansing Effort

The Problem: Organisations allocate less of project time to data cleansing when it realistically requires lots more.

Pitfall 2: Lack of Business Ownership

The Problem: Treating master data as an IT problem rather than a business accountability.

Pitfall 3: Ignoring Non-SAP Systems

The Problem: Focusing solely on SAP data while critical master data resides in other solutions.

Pitfall 4: "We'll Clean It Up After Go-Live"

The Problem: Deferring data quality issues to post-implementation, where they're exponentially harder and more expensive to address.

Pitfall 5: Treating Master Data and AI as Separate Initiatives

The Problem: Running master data improvement and AI development as independent projects with different teams and timelines.

Pitfall 6: Building Point Solutions Instead of Platforms

The Problem: Creating custom data quality tools and integration scripts that become technical debt and can't scale to AI demands.

 

The Business Case: Why Data Readiness Pays

While master data initiatives require investment, the ROI is compelling when you consider both S/4HANA and AI value realisation:

Quantifiable Benefits:

  • 30-40% reduction in S/4HANA migration timeline and risk
  • 50-70% improvement in AI model accuracy and reliability
  • 25-35% reduction in manual data reconciliation efforts
  • 60% faster time-to-deployment for new AI use cases
  • Elimination of duplicate payments and customer records
  • 20-30% reduction in data-related support incidents

Strategic Benefits:

  • Foundation for data-driven and AI-powered decision making
  • Enablement of real-time analytics and predictive insights
  • Improved customer experience through unified views and personalisation
  • Regulatory compliance and audit readiness for AI governance
  • Competitive advantage through faster, more reliable AI innovation
  • Reduced risk of AI bias and ethical issues from poor data

AI-Specific Value:

  • Data scientists spend less time on data preparation, more on model development
  • Higher model accuracy translates to better business outcomes
  • Faster experimentation and iteration on AI use cases
  • Reduced risk of AI project failure due to data quality issues
  • Ability to scale AI across the enterprise with confidence

The question isn't whether you can afford to invest in master data readiness it's whether you can afford not to, given the risks to your S/4HANA transformation and the potential lost value from underperforming AI initiatives.

 

Summary: The Path Forward

Master data readiness is not a one-time project it's a continuous discipline that requires the right strategy, platforms, processes, and people. As S/4HANA transformations and AI initiatives converge, the quality of your master data becomes the single most important factor in determining success or failure.

Key Takeaways:

  1. Data Quality is the AI Foundation - Poor master data quality will undermine AI initiatives regardless of how sophisticated your models are. Garbage in, garbage out applies more than ever.
  2. Unify SAP and Non-SAP Data - AI requires comprehensive data across your entire enterprise. Build a unified master data strategy that spans all systems, not just SAP.
  3. Start Early, Start Strategic - Data readiness must begin before S/4HANA migration and AI deployment, not after. Build the foundation correctly from day one.
  4. Governance is Non-Negotiable - Technology alone won't solve data quality problems. Establish clear ownership, stewardship, and accountability for data quality.
  5. Think Platform, Not Point Solutions - Invest in enterprise master data management platforms that can scale with your S/4HANA and AI ambitions. Solutions like SAP Business Data Cloud can help accelerate your AI journey by providing the unified, governed data foundation AI demands.
  6. Measure What Matters - Establish data quality KPIs and track AI model performance improvements attributable to better data. Make the business case visible and ongoing.
  7. Integration is Key - Build robust integration architecture that can handle real-time AI demands while maintaining data quality and governance across SAP and non-SAP systems.

 

Conclusion

The convergence of S/4HANA transformations and AI adoption creates both unprecedented opportunity and a critical dependency: the quality of your master data. Organisations that recognise this dependency and invest in comprehensive master data readiness will accelerate their transformations, de-risk their migrations, and unlock the full potential of AI-driven insights and automation.

Those that continue to treat master data as an afterthought will find themselves trapped in a cycle of rework, delayed value realisation, and unreliable AI outputs regardless of how much they invest in cutting-edge technology.

The foundation of digital transformation and AI success isn't S/4HANA or machine learning platforms it's the quality, consistency, and accessibility of your master data across your entire enterprise landscape. Whether you leverage SAP Business Data Cloud, another master data management solution, or build your own platform, the imperative remains the same: unified, governed, AI-ready master data is non-negotiable.
